2606.15835.title: Wasserstein Convergence of ODE-Based Samplers in Decentralized Diffusion Model via Velocity Field Decomposition
2606.15835.paper_summary: Diffusion models have achieved impressive empirical success in generative tasks, and their convergence theory is now relatively well understood. Motivated by privacy and scalability, recent decentralized diffusion architectures replace a single global velocity field with multiple local experts and a routing mechanism, yielding a sampling dynamics with stochastic expert switching that falls outside standard diffusion convergence analyses. In this work, We study a decentralized diffusion framework with stochastic velocity fields and ODE-based sampling. We establish a convergence guarantee in Wasserstein-2 distance, showing that the distribution of the $N$-step discretization converges to the analytical solution at rate $\mathcal{O}(N^{-1/2}+\varepsilon)$ in $W_2$, where $\varepsilon$ captures the neural approximation errors. To our knowledge, this is the first $W_2$ convergence result for decentralized diffusion models with an ODE-based sampling scheme.
2606.15835.paper_summary_zh: 擴散模型在生成任務中取得了令人印象深刻的實證成功，並且它們的收斂理論現在相對較為清晰。出於隱私和可擴展性的考量，最近的去中心化擴散架構用多個局部專家和路由機制取代了單一的全局速度場，從而產生了一種具有隨機專家切換的取樣動力學，這超出了標準擴散收斂分析的範疇。在這項工作中，我們研究了一個具有隨機速度場和基於常微分方程（ODE）取樣的去中心化擴散框架。我們在Wasserstein-2距離中建立了一個收斂保證，顯示出$N$步離散化的分佈以速率$\mathcal{O}(N^{-1/2}+\varepsilon)$收斂到解析解，其中$\varepsilon$捕捉了神經近似誤差。據我們所知，這是第一個針對具有基於ODE取樣方案的去中心化擴散模型的$W_2$收斂結果。
2606.15835.author: Chencheng Tang et.al.
2606.15835.authors: Chencheng Tang, Xuanyu Xue, Fangyikang Wang, Chao Zhang, Hubery Yin
